$schema: study-abroad-planner v0.3.0 CDU OT fieldwork & compliance

practice_education_hours.rationale: World Federation of Occupational Therapists (WFOT) mandate + Australian Minimum Competency Standards for New Graduate Occupational Therapists (OTBA 2018)
practice_education_hours.matches_mandate: 120 + 816 = 936, likely + simulation block hours to reach 1000
practice_education_hours.simulation_block_hours: 30 hours on-campus per relevant unit (24 hours for OCC311/OCC512/OCC513)

mandatory.note: If not cleared, cannot undertake placements and will not meet course requirements

mandatory.requirement: Working with Children Check (WWCC / Ochre Card in NT)
mandatory.note: Apply in your state/territory where you will be undertaking placement
Required before practical placements. Includes COVID-19 (subject to state/territory guidelines). Provide proof of exemption from medical doctor if applicable.
Some host organisations require this. May be additional to WWCC.

mandatory.examples: Equal Opportunity Training + COVID-19 Infection Control Training

pre_placement_compliance_requirements.estimated_total_compliance_cost_aud: 300-500 (police check + WWCC + immunisations + first aid + NDIS screening if needed)

placement_geo_constraints.duration: 5-8 weeks in regional or remote location
funding_options: CDU Scholarship (Faculty of Health Placements Central)
First Nations students: 'away from base' funding via CDU if eligible

on_campus_intensives_in_darwin.frequency: Once per semester for final two years

ahpra_student_registration.mechanism: CDU Faculty of Health registers all OT students with AHPRA under National Law
ahpra_student_registration.student_action_required: None — automatic upon enrolment in OT program

key_risks_for_user: 1000 practice hours is mandatory — refusal of placement offer may jeopardise course completion (per CDU handbook)
5-8 week rural/remote rotation is mandatory — must self-fund travel + accommodation
Police check / WWCC / immunisations must be valid AT TIME of each placement block (renewed annually for police)
NDIS Worker Screening may be required depending on host organisation — verify in advance
AHPRA student registration is automatic but students must comply with legislative requirements under National Law

before_applying: ★ Confirm current immunisation status (especially COVID) — gaps will need filling
★ Get police check early (3-4 weeks processing in some states)
★ Check NT Ochre Card (WWCC) requirements before arriving in Darwin
Budget ~$2,000-3,500 AUD for compliance + 1 rural placement over 2 years
during_study: Cannot defer immunisations / police check / WWCC — they must be valid for each placement block
If placement offer is refused, may delay graduation by 6-12 months (per CDU handbook)
